rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. YASMIN B SHAIKH AKA YASMIN SHAIKH
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 20, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF DISCOVER BANK'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL. Plaintiff Discover Bank's motion to set aside dismissal and enter judgment is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. The March 2, 2015 dismissal is vacated. Discover Bank is entitled to judgment in its favor and against defendant Yasmin Shaikh in the amount of $2,389.22 inclusive of attorney's fees and costs of suit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Discover Bank is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and states that the motion was not opposed and a proposed judgment that conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ERIC BOICE et al VS. HARCOURT GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILIT et al (Pltfs Challenge Judge Quidachay)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiffs' Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint, For An Order Dismissing Class Claims, And Approval Of Notice To Class Members
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 20, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS ERIC BOICE, VINETTA BOICE, YOWIE STROMBERG'S Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint, For An Order Dismissing Class Claims, And Approval Of Notice To Class Members. Plaintiffs' motion for leave to file a second amended complaint, for an order dismissing class claims, and for approval of notice to class members is granted in its entirety. Defendants Harcourt Group, LLC and Sojourn Properties, Inc. do not object to the dismissal of the class claims and in light of the affirmance of the denial of class certification and disapproval of the proposed class settlement, dismissing the class claims is the proper course. Plaintiffs' proposed notice to the class is factually accurate and reasonably notifies the recipients that they "may" but are not required to contact the Hooshmand Law Group, the firm that quite obviously knows quite a bit about the claims in this case. There is nothing unethical or improper about the statements in the notice about contacting the Hooshmand Law Group or another lawyer. Permitting plaintiffs to file an amended complaint adding Jennifer Hawkins as a plaintiff and four new causes of action comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings, particularly where, as here, no trial date has been set and the case is in the early stages of discovery. Nor have defendants identified any legal prejudice they would suffer by plaintiffs being permitted to file their proposed second amended complaint. The fact that Ms. Hawkins is being added by amendment instead of by joinder, intervention or consolidation with a separately filed lawsuit by Ms. Hawkins is immaterial and is not a reason to deny leave to file a second amended complaint. The face of the proposed second amended complaint and the prior orders in this case do not show that the claims sought to be alleged by Ms. Hawkins are time-barred. Per the American Pipe line of cases, the statute of limitations on Ms. Hawkins' claims were tolled from the date this case was filed until the remittitur issued by the Court of Appeal after its affirmance of the denial of plaintiff's motion for class certification. The California case law cited by defendants purporting that Ms. Hawkins may not avail herself of American Pipe tolling is inapposite. Falk v. Children's Hospital Los Angeles (2015) 237 Cal. App. 4th 1454 dealt with the situation where a party sought to bring a second class action lawsuit, not individual claims as Ms. Hawkins seeks to do. Perkins v. San Diego Gas & Electric Co. (2014) 225 Cal. App. 4th 492 held that the earlier class action case did not give notice to the defendants of the later individual claims sought to be pursued. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and specifies the papers that are to be filed nunc pro tunc as of May 17, 2018 and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILDA L WHITE VS. GIRISH SUBRAMANYAN MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 20, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFF WILDA WHITE'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Plaintiff Wilda White's application for the pro hac vice admission of Robert Ray is continued to July 16, 2018 to give defendants Girish Subramanyan and San Francisco Psychiatrist, Inc. the code-compliant amount of time to oppose the application if they truly intend to oppose what appears to be an application that (but for its service) is code-compliant and is unlikely to engender any opposition. Defendants must file and serve any substantive opposition they have to the application no later than July 2, 2018 and any further reply papers must be filed and served by July 9, 2018. E-service is both permitted and required. If defendants do not intend to file substantive opposition papers, judicial economy and civility strongly suggest that defendants so advise Ms. White and the court now so that the application can be granted without need for a continuance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling (or, in this case, state its lack of intent to file substantive opposition papers) must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KAI KOPP VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Amend The Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 20, 2018, Line 13, PLAINTIFF KAI KOPP'S Motion To Amend The Complaint. Plaintiff Kai Kopp's motion to file his proposed second amended complaint is granted on the conditions that: a) the filing of the amended complaint does not affect the City's pending motion for summary judgment which will remain on calendar as noticed and b) the trial date is continued to February 19, 2019 at 9:30am in department 206 and the only discovery that is permitted after the current discovery cutoff date is discovery propounded by defendant Latin American Baseball League to the new legal and factual allegations alleged by Mr. Kopp in his second amended complaint. If Mr. Kopp agrees to both conditions in writing or orally at the hearing, Mr. Kopp has leave to file his second amended complaint no later than June 26, 2018. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings at any time. The two conditions eliminate any possible legal prejudice to the defendants resulting from the granting of the motion. rulings.Case Title: JUDY CHU VS. STEVEN KAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Nunc Pro Tunc Directing The Clerk To File, And To Correct Filing Date To May 17, 2018 On: (1) Petition For Writ Of Mandate To Correct, Modify Or Partially Vacate Arbitration Award And (2) Request For Judicial Notice And (3) Declaration Of Richard J. Idell In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 20, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANTS GOLDEN STATE RENAISSANCE VENTURES, LLC, GSRV MANAGEMENT, LLC, GSRV I MANAGEMENT, LLC, GSRV II MANAGEMENT, LLC, GSRV III MANAGEMENT, LLC, SFBARC 5 MANAGEMENT, LLC, SFBARC 6 MANAGEMENT, LLC'S Motion For Order Nunc Pro Tunc Directing The Clerk To File, And To Correct Filing Date To May 17, 2018 On: (1) Petition For Writ Of Mandate To Correct, Modify Or Partially Vacate Arbitration Award And (2) Request For Judicial Notice And (3) Declaration Of Richard J. Idell In Support Thereof. GSRV Entities, SFBARC Entities and 3G Entities' motion for order nunc pro tunc directing the clerk to file certain papers as of May 17, 2018 is granted. Moving parties have shown good cause for the relief they seek. Consistent with the declaration of Glenda Cayabyab submitted in support of this motion, the court's records show that the sole reason why the papers presented for filing on May 17, 2018 and now sought to be filed nunc pro tunc as of that date were rejected for filing is that the clerk reviewing the papers believed that a court order was necessary for the papers to be filed because the papers included "3 new parties." While well-intentioned and perhaps in conformity with the instructions she received, the clerk should not have rejected the papers for the sole reason that she rejected them. No authority required the 3G Entities to obtain an order for them to join in the papers that were rejected. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY K LEE VS. HOSIE RICE LLP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 20, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANTS SPENCER HOSIE, HOSIE RICE LLP, DIANE RICE'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Hosie Rice LLP, Spencer Hosie, and Diane S. Rice's demurrer to the fourth cause of action for conversion alleged in the third amended complaint filed by plaintiff Anthony Lee is overruled. The allegations supporting the conversion claim are similar to the allegations that were found to be sufficient to allege a viable conversion claim in Sanowicz v. Bacal (2015) 234 Cal.App.4th 1027. Mr. Lee alleges that, per the Compensation Agreement, defendants owe him at least 12.72% of the 25 million XRP options and/or corresponding XRP; the defendants converted Mr. Lee's property by wrongfully withholding and refusing to provide Mr. Lee with his shares; defendants already disposed of 12.5 million shares by selling them without Mr. Lee's prior consent and sought to do the same with the remaining shares until Mr. Lee prevented them from doing so; and defendants defrauded Mr. Lee out of his rightful share by misrepresenting the actual percentage of fees due to him. Mr. Lee can state a viable conversion claim while still seeking an accounting regarding the exact amount at issue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Mr. Lee is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NP APARTMENTS, LLC. VS. ASSOCIATED MATERIALS, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set One And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 20, 2018, LINE 1, Motion 1 PLAINTIFF NP APARTMENTS, LLC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set One And For Sanctions Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One, is GRANTED and DENIED as follows: 12.1: defendant shall provide the address and telephone number of Kevin Carroll. 15.1: Second Affirmative Defense: DENIED; Fifth Affirmative Defense: GRANTED; Sixth Affirmative Defense: GRANTED; Seventh Affirmative Defense: GRANTED; Ninth Affirmative Defense: GRANTED; Tenth Affirmative Defense: GRANTED; thirteenth Affirmative Defense: DENIED; Par. 9: DENIED; Par. 17: GRANTED; Par. 52: DENIED; Par. 53: GRANTED; Par. 58: GRANTED; Par. 62: GRANTED; Par. 65: DENIED; Par. 67: GRANTED; Par. 69: GRANTED; Par. 71: GRANTED; Par. 72: GRANTED; Par. 73: GRANTED; Par. 92: DENIED; Par. 93: GRANTED; Par. 94: GRANTED; Par. 95: GRANTED; Par. 96: GRANTED; Par. 99: GRANTED; Par. 102: DENIED; Par. 105: DENIED; Par. 106: GRANTED; Par. 107: GRANTED; Par. 108: DENIED; Par. 109: GRANTED; Par. 110: GRANTED; Par. 13: GRANTED; Par. 114: DENIED; Par. 120: GRANTED; Par. 131: DENIED; Par. 132: DENIED; Par. 142: GRANTED; Par. 143: GRANTED; 15.1(b): GRANTED; 15.1(c): GRANTED; 17.1 (80-83): GRANTED. All requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = PC

rulings.Case Title: SEUNG KU KANG VS. KOREAN AMERICAN COMMUNITY CENTER OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 20, 2018, Line 12, DEFENDANTS SUNG HO CHO, YOUNG JIN BAE'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant Sung Ho Cho and Young Jin Bae's anti-SLAPP motion to strike the sole cause of action for defamation in the complaint filed by plaintiff Seung Ku Kang is denied. Mr. Kang's asserted second cause of action for punitive damages is not a separate claim from his first cause of action for defamation for purposes of this motion. Mr. Cho and Mr. Bae satisfied their first prong burden of showing that the advertisement in the Korea Times was made in a public forum in connection with an issue of public interest (CCP 425.16 (e)(3).) Weinberg v. Feisel (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1122 is distinguishable. There, the defendant believed that the plaintiff stole his token and ran an advertisement in a newsletter regarding the plaintiff in an attempt to oust plaintiff from the token association. Weinberg involved a private matter-one person stealing a token from another. This case involves a public matter-one person allegedly stealing funds from a community organization that affects numerous persons. Mr. Kang satisfied his second prong burden of showing that his defamation claim has minimal merit. The evidence on this motion shows that Mr. Kang can prove a prima facie case of libel. Defendants' objection to paragraph 7 of Mr. Kang's declaration as lacking foundation is sustained. The declarations of Mr. Cho and Mr. Bae, however, show that they are members of the board of the Korean American Community Center and they took a responsible part in the publication. Mr. Kang's declaration, which denies the charges, presents sufficient evidence of malice to require that the malice issue be resolved by the trier of fact. Even if Mr. Kang is a limited public figure, which is disputed because he did not voluntarily thrust himself into a public issue, his declaration is sufficient to show a triable issue regarding actual malice. Mr. Kang's fee request is denied because the motion was not frivolous. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Kang is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
